cited_work: Ten Arguments in Search of a Philosopher: Averroes and Aquinas in Ficino's Platonic Theology
relevant_topic: Scope of SCG 2 as source for all major features of PT Book 15
claim_summary: Almost all the prominent items in Ficino's discussion in PT Book 15 — including the four Averroist arguments (A1–A4), three-part excursus (E1–3), contextual material (K1–3), and three concluding Averroist positions (C1–3) — can be found in SCG 2.46–81, not just 2.59.
exact_quote: Almost all the prominent items in Ficino's discussion can be found in these chapters of SCG 2: the act and esse of the composite substance; participation; the material mode of reception; infinite intellectual power; the individuation of species and images; the teacher's vanishing knowledge; distinct powers of cognition and instinctive judgement; the locations of psychic functions in the brain; the cosmology of celestial spheres; the substantiality of intellectual entities; a different type of being for such entities; and intellection of all the knowledge of the human species.
notes: Expands the SCG source from 2.59.3-6 to the broader range 2.46-81, showing the dependence extends beyond the anti-Averroist summary.
